hypothesis: Applying a pulsed current waveform (100 ms on at 498 mA cm−2, 100 ms off; average 249 mA cm−2) to a reactive carbon electrolyzer operated at the champion electrolyte composition (1.5 M KHCO3 + 0.75 M K2CO3, total 3 M K+) will increase reactor yield by ≥20% relative to continuous DC operation at 249 mA cm−2 by improving in situ CO2 mass transport and gas-bubble detachment, thereby raising JCO while maintaining or improving UCO2.
